title: Meet the Astronomer Tracking UFOs
summary: Dr. Beatrice Villarroel, an astronomer, discusses her work on Project VASCO searching for vanishing stars and transient objects in astronomical data, including anomalous findings from July 1952 that coincide with the Washington DC UFO flap. She also describes her new Exoprobe project to detect non-human artifacts in Earth orbit and her involvement with the European Crash Retrieval Initiative.

acronyms.definition: Modern term for UFOs that the guest criticizes as too broad and poorly defined for scientific investigation
Scientific effort to detect signs of intelligent extraterrestrial life, traditionally through radio signals
Alleged secret committee of scientists, military leaders, and government officials formed to investigate UFO activity

acronyms.expansion: Vanishing and Appearing Sources during a Century of Observations
acronyms.definition: Project comparing astronomical images from the 1950s to modern images to find stars or objects that have vanished

events.description: Washington DC UFO flap occurred on July 19-20 and July 26-27, with multiple UFO sightings over the capital prompting a call between Truman and Edward J. Ruppelt
Dr. Villarroel found anomalous transient objects in Mount Palomar images from July 19 (triple transient) and July 27 (five objects in narrow band) that coincide with Washington flap dates
Don Menzel stopped Harvard astronomers from conducting their sky survey, which did not resume until 1967
Atomic Energy Act of 1954 allegedly created UFO secrecy according to David Grusch
Jeff Marcy was involved in a sexual harassment scandal and subsequently cancelled
Dr. Villarroel published paper about nine transients discovery with Jeff Marcy, same week National Academy of Sciences removed Marcy
Nimitz UFO encounter detected when new forward-looking infrared system from Raytheon was installed

key_claims.claim: Multiple transient objects appearing and vanishing were found in astronomical images from July 19 and July 27, 1952, coinciding exactly with the dates of the Washington DC UFO flap

Don Menzel became director of Harvard Observatory 2-3 months after helping the US Air Force debunk the Washington 1952 UFO flap, then destroyed one-third of the photographic plates and stopped the Harvard sky survey from 1953-1967

The Baltic Sea anomaly was found covered in burned biological material, which is unusual because there's no volcanic activity in that area, and scientists who claimed it was just a rock never studied the actual samples or published a paper
When the world's largest optical telescope (10.4m Gran Telescopio Canarias) was pointed at the positions of the nine vanishing transient objects, nothing was found at those locations
J. Allen Hynek worked under Don Menzel at Harvard through Operation Moonwatch, which had 36 UFO reports that have been found
Robert Sarbacher was running the Harvard undersea observatory and allegedly created UFO secrecy through the Atomic Energy Act of 1954
JFK sent a letter to CIA acting director John McCone asking for data on unknowns due to concerns about Soviet perception of objects in their airspace
The European Crash Retrieval Initiative is currently working on a cold case that cannot be disclosed publicly
Radio SETI has failed, so scientists should look for artifacts and probes from advanced civilizations instead
